title: Alignment brief for competing exec goals
description: Real-world PM scenario on alignment brief for competing exec goals with conflicting stakeholder incentives and incomplete data.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at CostBeacon, a Series B finops company (176 employees, $16.1M ARR). Your immediate assignment is alignment brief for competing exec goals. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Optimize for repeatable outcomes over one-off wins that do not scale.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 23863
New logo adds: 2892
Activation rate: 24%
30-day retention: 44%
NPS: 7 (n=61)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 7%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: -6 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: -5 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Samir Carter): "I need a plan sales can explain in live calls next week, not a roadmap promise."
VP Engineering (Riya Gupta): "Scope drift, not coding speed, is the main reason timelines slip. Lock boundaries now."
Head of Customer Success (Riya Bennett): "Trust loss shows up before churn in expansion conversations."
Commercial note: 2 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 33 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Pinecrest Retail (Operations Lead): "We see value quickly once configured, but the first session still feels too heavy."
Keystone Legal (IT Manager): "We needed clearer ownership between implementation and support during rollout."
Harbor Wholesale (Director of Product): "I would expand if reporting and error states were easier for non-technical users."
Data gap: no structured interviews from churned accounts in the last 30 days.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 32
Median cycle time: 23 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 76%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 6
Release rollback events last quarter: 1
Average time to resolve escalations: 26 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Map each audience to the message they need and why.
submissionFields.label: Audience and Intent

submissionFields.hint: Write the core narrative that aligns stakeholders on action.
submissionFields.label: Core Narrative

submissionFields.hint: Predict objections from key stakeholders and how you will respond.
submissionFields.label: Expected Pushback

submissionFields.hint: Draft the memo or all-hands update you would send.
submissionFields.label: Message Draft

submissionFields.hint: Explain how you will collect feedback and close open issues.
submissionFields.label: Follow-up and Feedback Loop

rubric.name: Audience Fit
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is messaging adapted to the needs and incentives of each audience?

Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are claims grounded in evidence with transparent caveats?

expertSolution.content: Executives need decision logic and downside exposure, delivery teams need concrete scope and ownership, and customer-facing teams need timing and expectation framing. Tailor message depth by audience without changing the core narrative.

Recommendation: align on a staged plan because the data is mixed, not broken. Activation is 24% and retention is 44%, while NPS (7, n=61) and 33 escalations indicate trust risk if execution quality slips.

Expect commercial pressure from Samir Carter around the 2 deal pipeline, technical caution from Riya Gupta on dependency risk (6 unresolved), and customer-impact pressure from Riya Bennett due to escalation volume. Address each with explicit thresholds and review dates.

Team, we are moving to a bounded plan that prioritizes reliability and measurable customer value. Baseline today is 23863 active accounts, activation 24%, retention 44%, and on-time delivery 76%. We will publish weekly progress and revisit scope at the midpoint checkpoint.

Hold a structured Q&A within 24 hours, capture unresolved questions in a decision log, and assign owner plus due date for each item. Publish weekly updates that close the loop on objections raised by Sales, Engineering, and Customer Success.
